pi_items.source: Maritime visibility standards confirm 1.5 nautical miles represents poor/restricted visibility typical of fog conditions. References: https://www.turkishstraits.com/info/visibilitytable and maritime safety regulations.

Beaufort scale Force 4 is a valid meteorological classification representing wind speed of 11-15 knots (20-28 km/h). References: https://www.weather.gov/mfl/beaufort and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beaufort_scale

MV Pacific Venture is a real container feeder vessel (IMO 9743045) registered in Dominica, built in 2016, with 598 TEU container capacity. References: https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/details/ships/shipid:3573549/mmsi:325111400/imo:9743045/vessel:PACIFIC_VENTURE and https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/9743045

Solent center coordinates are approximately 50.7853°N, -1.2903°W. Metadata value differs by only 0.0023°, which equals approximately 0.26 km. This places the vessel very close to the Solent center. References: https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/6146/solent

Solent center longitude is -1.2903°W. Metadata value differs by 0.0953°, placing the vessel approximately 8.8 km east of Solent center, still within the regional maritime area. References: https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/6146/solent

FV Morning Star is a real fishing vessel name used by multiple commercial fishing operations, including F/V Morning Star operating in Alaskan Pollock fishery and other Morning Star fishing vessels registered in various countries. References: https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/7940118 and https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/details/ships/shipid:4259946/mmsi:316001949
All registered vessels named FV Morning Star are classified as fishing vessels. References: https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/7940118

MV Solent Guardian is a real vessel registered in the United Kingdom (MMSI 235096443). References: https://www.vesselfinder.com/vessels/details/235096443 and https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/details/ships/235096443

MV Solent Guardian is actually a Coastal Survey Vessel built in 2013, operated by Briggs Marine for nearshore survey work. Multiple sources confirm it is a survey vessel, not a tanker. References: https://www.briggsmarine.com/services/vessel-charter/solent-guardian/, https://www.offshorewind.biz/vessels/solent-guardian/, https://www.fleetmon.com/vessels/solent-guardian_0_8201545/

SY Dorado is a real sailing yacht name used for multiple vessels, including Jeanneau Sun Odyssey 419 Dorado (available for charter in Croatia) and other sailing yachts. References: https://www.boattrader.com/boats/make-dorado/ and https://www.yachtworld.com/boats-for-sale/make-dorado/
Multiple sailing yachts are registered with names including 'Dorado', confirming this is a valid yacht designation. References: https://www.boattrader.com/boats/make-dorado/

pi_items.verified_value: Rules 5, 6, 7, 8, 19 all exist and are relevant to collision avoidance
pi_items.source: COLREGS (Convention on the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972) confirms: Rule 5 (Lookout), Rule 6 (Safe Speed), Rule 7 (Risk of Collision), Rule 8 (Action to Avoid Collision), and Rule 19 (Conduct in Restricted Visibility) are all real and applicable to fog/collision scenarios. References: https://www.imo.org/en/About/Conventions/Pages/COLREG.aspx and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Regulations_for_Preventing_Collisions_at_Sea

The Solent is a real strait separating the Isle of Wight from mainland England, located at approximately 50.7853°N, 1.2903°W. References: https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/6146/solent

privileged_info_summary: The collision reconstruction scenario is based on real-world maritime locations and regulations. The Solent is an actual UK strait at coordinates approximately 50.7853°N, 1.2903°W, with the scenario positioned at 50.783°N, 1.195°W (within the regional area). Visibility of 1.5 nautical miles and weather Force 4 (Beaufort scale) are realistic maritime conditions. The scenario references real vessels: MV Pacific Venture is an actual container feeder vessel (IMO 9743045), FV Morning Star is a real fishing vessel name in commercial use, and SY Dorado is a real sailing yacht name. However, MV Solent Guardian is correctly named as a real vessel (UK-registered survey vessel) but is incorrectly classified in metadata as a 'Tanker' when it is actually a Coastal Survey Vessel. COLREGS Rules 5, 6, 7, 8, and 19 are all authentic maritime collision avoidance regulations with Rule 19 specifically addressing restricted visibility scenarios. The scenario data itself is synthetically constructed by the task setup script rather than sourced from real incident databases.
